title: Icacorea proctorii
taxonomy: Icacorea proctorii is classified within the family Icacinaceae, a small family of flowering plants. This family is part of the order Icacinales, which is a distinct order within the clade Asterids. The order Icacinales is further classified within the clade Campanulids, which also includes other orders such as the Campanulales and the Aquifoliales. The clade Campanulids is part of the larger clade Euasterids, a group of flowering plants that also includes the clades Gentianidae and Cornales. Icacorea proctorii's classification is an example of the complex and evolving relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Icacorea proctorii, also known as the Proctor's Icacorea, is a plant species of the family Icacinaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Icacorea proctorii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. Traditionally, Icacorea proctorii is used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
